summary: DHH recounts the celebration of Ruby on Rails' first birthday at OSCON in Portland, noting the overwhelming positive reception from the community. He thanks the O'Reilly team for giving Rails prominent placement with tutorial, session, and keynote slots. He reflects on the momentum building around Rails, with multiple conferences, the upcoming 1.0 release, and several books in production, all within just one year of the framework's existence.
key_points: Ruby on Rails celebrated its first birthday at OSCON with strong community enthusiasm and multiple speaking slots
O'Reilly gave Ruby a dedicated track at OSCON, signaling mainstream recognition of the language and framework
Rails momentum continued to build with upcoming appearances at JAOO, Web 2.0, RubyConf, and EuroOSCON, plus 7+ books in production
The 1.0 release was imminent, marking a major milestone after just one year
money_quotes: It was an overwhelming lovefest for what the Ruby on Rails community has created within this tiny sliver of time.
It's so exciting to see that after all the good times we've been enjoying through the year still haven't brought us to a peak.
There's so much more to come.
key_insight: Just one year after its release, Ruby on Rails had already generated enough momentum — conferences, books, community enthusiasm — to signal it was far from peaking.
